title: Metachronous Isolated Splenic Metastasis in a Young Patient With Renal Cell Carcinoma: Case Report and Literature Review.
author_string: Costantini, Manuela, Tuderti, Gabriele, Minisola, Francesco, Pompeo, Vincenzo, Sentinelli, Steno et al.
abstract: Splenic metastasis is uncommon and is usually associated with widespread disease.<sup>1,2</sup> Isolated splenic metastases from renal cancer are also rarer and are often an incidental finding. This eventuality may turn into a dangerous scenario due to a spontaneous splenic rupture leading to sudden death.<sup>2,3</sup> At the best of our knowledge, only few cases of metastasis from renal cell carcinoma (RCC) have been documented in the literature.<sup>4-11</sup> We hereby present a literature review of these cases and report a case of isolated splenic metastasis in a young man on active follow-up for a clear cell RCC clear cell Renal Cell Carcinoma (ccRCC).
